name: Sierra de La Sagra Loop
name_original: Rodeando la Sierra de La Sagra desde Huéscar
description: This difficult 35.3-mile touring cycling route around Sierra de La Sagra from Huéscar offers rugged mountain scenery and challenging terrain
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Huéscar traverse a landscape characterized by mountainous terrain, natural parks, and geological formations. The region, part of the Granada Geopark, features prominent ranges like the Sierra de La Sagra and the Sierra de Castril Natural Park. These areas offer varied cycling experiences, from routes with moderate inclines to those with substantial elevation gains. The terrain includes both paved and unpaved segments, suitable for different bike types. Best touring cycling routes around Huéscar The most popular touring cycling route is Sierra de La Sagra Loop, a 35.3 miles (56.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 7 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain around the prominent Sierra de La Sagra mountain range. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Circular from Huéscar via Embalse de San Clemente, a moderate 23.4 miles (37.7 km) path. This route offers a scenic circular journey passing by the Embalse de San Clemente. Local touring cyclists also love the Puntal de las Buitreras from Santiago de la Espada - circular gravel via the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas, a 25.5 miles (41.1 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas Natural Park, often completed in about 3 hours 57 minutes. Touring cycling around Huéscar is defined by mountainous terrain, natural parks, and geological formations, offering routes for various ability levels. The routes in Huéscar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 170 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Huéscar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Huéscar?
A: Huéscar offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 30 recorded options. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult, ensuring there's a suitable path for most cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Huéscar?
A: The terrain around Huéscar is diverse, characterized by mountainous landscapes, natural parks like the Sierra de La Sagra and Sierra de Castril, and geological formations within the Granada Geopark. You'll find a mix of paved and unpaved segments, suitable for various bike types, with routes ranging from moderate inclines to substantial elevation gains.

Q: What are the best times of year for touring cycling in Huéscar?
A: The most favorable months for touring cycling in Huéscar are June and September. During these periods, the weather is generally pleasant, making for ideal conditions to explore the region's diverse routes.

Q: Are there routes suitable for less experienced touring cyclists in Huéscar?
A: Yes, Huéscar offers several routes suitable for less experienced touring cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1054959945/circular-from-huescar-via-embalse-de-san-clemente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular from Huéscar via Embalse de San Clemente&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 37.7 km path that offers a scenic journey with manageable elevation changes.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Huéscar?
A: The touring cycling routes around Huéscar are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 3.5 stars. Over 170 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the region's natural beauty and diverse cycling experiences.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Huéscar?
A: Yes, there are several circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1054959945/circular-from-huescar-via-embalse-de-san-clemente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular from Huéscar via Embalse de San Clemente&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a scenic loop passing by the Embalse de San Clemente. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045607265/vega-del-rio-orce-circular-from-galera-geoparque-de-granada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega del Rio Orce — circular from Galera — Geoparque de Granada&lt;/a&gt;, offering a circular journey through the Granada Geopark.

Q: Can I find touring cycling routes that pass through the Granada Geopark?
A: Absolutely. Huéscar is situated within the Granada Geopark, and many touring cycling routes traverse this geologically significant landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045607265/vega-del-rio-orce-circular-from-galera-geoparque-de-granada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega del Rio Orce — circular from Galera — Geoparque de Granada&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering a moderate ride through this unique area.

Q: What natural landmarks or points of interest can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region is rich in natural landmarks. You can encounter impressive gorges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5297752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castril Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail through the mountains&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6226243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega of the Orce River&lt;/a&gt; also offers scenic views. Additionally, the Sierra de La Sagra and Sierra de Castril Natural Park provide dramatic mountainous backdrops and unique rock formations.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced riders will find plenty of challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1039211026/sierra-de-la-sagra-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de La Sagra Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 56.8 km route with significant elevation gain around the prominent Sierra de La Sagra mountain range. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996244853/puntal-de-las-buitreras-from-santiago-de-la-espada-circular-gravel-via-the-p-n-de-las-sierras-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de las Buitreras from Santiago de la Espada&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 41.1 km gravel route through a natural park.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass through historic villages?
A: While specific routes are not detailed, the region around Huéscar is known for charming villages like Castril, which features ancient castles on rocks and a pleasant atmosphere. Many routes will likely pass near or through such historic sites, offering opportunities to explore local culture and gastronomy.

Q: Can I find touring cycling routes with significant elevation changes?
A: Huéscar's mountainous terrain ensures many routes feature significant elevation changes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1039211026/sierra-de-la-sagra-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de La Sagra Loop&lt;/a&gt; includes over 700 meters of ascent, providing a challenging experience for those seeking substantial climbs.
tips:
Town located at the foot of the Sierra de la Encantada, within what is known as the Altiplano of Granada.

The municipality is located at an altitude of 952 meters and in it stands out the church of Collegiate Church of Santa María la Mayor in Gothic-Renaissance style from the beginning of the 16th century.

Huéscar also has an incredible natural setting where you can appreciate gigantic sequoias.
Small hermitage located at the foot of the Sierra de La Sagra. The space has a recreational area with large stone tables where you can take a break on the route in an area surrounded by nature.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging touring cycling route typically takes around 4 hours and 6 minutes to complete. Keep in mind that this is an estimated duration and actual time may vary based on your fitness level, breaks, and the demanding terrain.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is categorized as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness'. Some segments are unpaved and challenging, potentially requiring you to push your bike. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route navigates the mountainous Sierra de La Sagra, featuring rugged, pine-covered landscapes and deep ravines. Expect a mix of surfaces, as some segments are unpaved and difficult to ride, characteristic of a 'Hard bike ride' classification.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is designed as a loop, starting and ending in Huéscar, allowing you to round the Sierra de La Sagra.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Huéscar. While specific parking details aren't provided, Huéscar is the logical starting point for this circular tour. You would typically find parking within the town itself.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience awe-inspiring mountainous scenery within the protected Sierra de La Sagra. Notable points of interest include the Collegiate Church of Santa María la Mayor of Huéscar and the Hermitage of the Holy Martyrs of the Mountain. The area also features unique landscape elements like screes up to 400 meters high.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for challenging conditions, spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle this route, offering milder temperatures than the peak of summer or winter.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra de La Sagra is a natural area, specific regulations regarding dogs on this particular touring cycling route are not detailed. It's always best to check local signage or official park guidelines for leash requirements and access restrictions before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this area?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de La Sagra is a protected natural area within the European Network of Protected Natural Areas. While there are no explicit mentions of permits or entrance fees for cycling this route, it's always advisable to respect the natural environment and adhere to any local regulations.

faqs.question: Are there water sources available along the way?
faqs.answer: Water availability along this challenging route is limited. It is crucial to stock up on sufficient water before embarking on your tour, as reliable water points may not be readily available.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other official trails, including the Gran Senda Primeros Pobladores, El Camino Espiritual del Sur, and Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Granada).
